
Notes on how to update libtpms to a newer version
-------------------------------------------------

We keep track of the external sources here in /vendor/libtpms.  The 'current'
directory is where we apply the OpenSSL patches, where as the version subdirs 
like '0.9.0' are tags of 'current' for the corresponding version.

Unlike EDK2, we do take the sources as-is, so updating 'current' is just a matter
of applying all the changes made between the two versions.  No cleanup steps needed.

For general information about vendor branches, see:
  http://svnbook.red-bean.com/en/1.8/svn.advanced.vendorbr.html 


Updating the vendor branch
--------------------------

The goal here is to make vendor/libtpms/current an exact replica of the 
current libtpms tarball.  We use diff to find files and directories that
are added and removed.  There are other ways.

Steps (y.y.y = previous version, x.x.x = new version):

    0. Make sure /vendor/libtpms is correctly tagged, tag if needed.

    1. Download the tarball of the release and unpack it in /tmp/.  You should 
       get a directory /tmp/libtpms-x.x.x containing the sources.

    2. Create a diff between 'current' and the tarball:
           cd vendor/libtpms
           diff -ruN --binary current /tmp/libtpms-x.x.x > /tmp/y.y.y-x.x.x.diff

    3. Apply the patch to vendor/libtpms/current:
           svn patch /tmp/y.y.y-x.x.x.diff
           
    4. Check that the tarball and 'current' matches, copy over and add any 
       missing files (typically binaries):
           cd current
           diff -r . /tmp/libtpms-x.x.x
           cd ..
           svn st current | grep -F "?"     # or 'less' instead of grep

    5. Copy /tmp/libtpms-x.x.x/ on top of current/ to ensure there are no line 
       ending issues or such.

       Windows:
           xcopy /s \tmp\libtpms-x.x.x\* current\

       Unix:
           cp ...

    6. Redo step 4 just to be sure.

    7. Commit current:
           svn ci -m "vendor/libtpms: Updated to x.x.x. bugref:xxxx"
           svn st       # (check if we missed anything)

    8. Tag it:
           svn copy -m "vendor/libtpms: Tagging y.y.y. bugref:xxxx" \
               "^/vendor/libtpms/current" \
               "^/vendor/libtpms/x.x.x"

P.S. There is no real need to check out the tags unless you want to look at 
     the actual sources or something.


Updating the code in the VBox tree
----------------------------------

Update the source in the VBox tree can be done in several ways.  


Variant I (y.y.y = previous version, x.x.x = new version):

    1. Start with a fresh libtpms copy:
           svn copy -m "libs: Copied unmodified libtpms vx.x.x sources from vendor branch. bugref:xxxx" \
               "^/vendor/libtpms/current" \
               "^/trunk/src/libs/libtpms-x.x.x"
           svn up src/libs
   
    2. Apply our changes:
           cd src/libs/libtpms-x.x.x
           svn merge  "^/vendor/libtpms/y.y.y" "^/trunk/src/libs/libtpms-y.y.y" .

    3. Resolve merge conflicts and make it build.

    4. Commit:
           svn ci -m "libtpms-x.x.x: Applied and adjusted our libtpms changes to x.x.x.  bugref:xxxx"

    5. Modify src/Makefile.kmk and Config.kmk to enable using the new version.

    P.S. All the merging and building fixes does not need to be one commit.


Variant II (y.y.y = previous version, x.x.x = new version):

    1. Copy our current libtpms to the new location:
          svn copy -m "libs: Copied our libtpms vy.y.y in preparation for vx.x.x upgrade. bugref:xxxx" \
              "^/trunk/src/libs/libtpms-y.y.y" \
              "^/trunk/src/libs/libtpms-x.x.x"

    2. Apply the upstream libtpms changes:
          cd src/libs/libtpms-x.x.x
          svn merge "^/vendor/libtpms/y.y.y" "^/vendor/libtpms/x.x.x" .

    3. Resolve merge conflicts and build it build.

    4. Commit:
           svn ci -m "libtpms-x.x.x: Applied and adjusted our libtpms changes to x.x.x.  bugref:xxxx"

    5. Modify src/Makefile.kmk and Config.kmk to enable using the new version.

    P.S. All the merging and building fixes does not need to be one commit.


The difference between the two variants is that the first one is likely to be 
more gentle with subversion space.  The merge conflict resolving and such is
should be more or less the same.   OTOH, the second variant will better retain 
our change history and is the one described in the svnbook link at the top of 
this document.
